underlying: Republican margin of victory, 2026 Mississippi U.S. Senate general election (Nov 3, 2026). Incumbent Cindy Hyde-Smith (R) seeking second full term.
method: Anchor: 2018 special Hyde-Smith +7.2 vs Espy. 2020 Hyde-Smith +10.0 vs Espy. 2024 Wicker +25.6 vs Pinkins. 2024 Trump won MS by ~17pts. Hyde-Smith chronically underperforms generic R; Wicker is a stronger candidate. Now faces D Scott Colom (Lowndes Cnty DA, well-funded). Modeled R margin ~ N(13, 6).
notes: Hyde-Smith historically runs 5-10pts behind state PVI (won 2020 by 10 in a Trump+16 state). Trump endorsed her in primary; she won 80.8% in March 2026 R primary. D nominee Scott Colom is a well-known DA — credible. MS has high Black population (~38%) meaning D floor is high. 2024 Trump+17 sets baseline, but Hyde-Smith underperforms ~5pts. Model: ~13. Wider sigma due to Hyde-Smith's persistent underperformance and Colom's profile. Kalshi YES asks: P3 0.86, P11 0.56, P13 0.48, P15 0.41 — market median ~12-13, aligned.
